stories.title: Rankings Overhaul: Sinner Leads by 4,970 Points, Zverev Jumps Alcaraz, Fery Rockets to No. 36

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Post-Wimbledon ATP rankings. Let's go:

No. 1 Sinner leads by 4,970 points.
No. 2 Zverev — overtook Alcaraz for the first time.
No. 3 Alcaraz — drops one.
No. 36 Fery — up from No. 114. SEVENTY-EIGHT spots in one week. 🎾

#ATP #TennisFanrecap #Wimbledon

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
WTA rankings after Wimbledon:

— Noskova: career-high No. 7 (was No. 12) 🏆
— Swiatek: fell from No. 3 to No. 8
— Gauff: up three spots to No. 4
— Muchova: career-high No. 6

Wimbledon reshapes the tour. Every. Single. Year.

#WTA #TennisFanrecap #Wimbledon

article.headline: The Post-Wimbledon Rankings Reveal a Sport Reshaped in Two Weeks

research.facts:
Rankings Overhaul Post-Wimbledon 2026
**Decision:** NEW STORY — Rankings are refreshed weekly; post-Wimbledon edition is always significant given the large points at stake. The specific numbers (4,970-point Sinner gap, Zverev overtaking Alcaraz, Fery +78 spots) were not published before July 13.
**Sources:**
- Yardbarker, Jul 13, 2026: "ATP Rankings Update: Jannik Sinner Maintains Dominance as World No. 1 as Alexander Zverev Jumps Carlos Alcaraz" — confirmed No. 1 lead and Zverev's jump to No. 2
- beIN Sports, Jul 13, 2026: "Zverev second in ATP rankings behind Sinner after Wimbledon" — confirms Zverev/Alcaraz swap
- ATP Tour, Jul 13, 2026: Official rankings release + "PIF ATP Rankings Mover of the Week: Arthur Fery" — confirms Fery No. 114 to No. 36
- Philstar.com, Jul 13, 2026: "Noskova moves into WTA top 10 after Wimbledon triumph" — career high No. 7
- Just Women's Sports, Jul 13, 2026: Full WTA rankings breakdown — Gauff No. 4, Swiatek No. 8, Muchova No. 6
- Sports Illustrated, Jul 13: "50 Parting Thoughts from 2026 Wimbledon" — contextual analysis
**Key Facts (all HIGH confidence):**
- Sinner No. 1, leads Zverev by 4,970 points (Yardbarker + beIN Sports — 2 sources)
- Zverev No. 2, overtook Alcaraz, who dropped to No. 3 (beIN Sports + Yardbarker — 2 sources)
- Fery No. 114 to No. 36, +78 spots, new British No. 1 (ATP Tour official + Olympics.com — 2 sources)
- Noskova WTA No. 7 career high, up 5 spots (Philstar.com + Just Women's Sports — 2 sources)
- Swiatek fell from No. 3 to No. 8, dropped 5 spots (Just Women's Sports + Philstar.com — 2 sources)
- Gauff up 3 spots to No. 4 (Just Women's Sports — 1 source; consistent with Wimbledon SF result)
- Muchova career-high No. 6 (Just Women's Sports — 1 source; consistent with finalist result)
**Freshness:** Rankings released July 13. Fully fresh.

stories.title: "Let Me Get Drunk" — Noskova's Viral Moment and What She Plans to Do with the Platform

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Noskova wins Wimbledon. Gets asked about celebrations.

"Let me get drunk."

No script. No media training. Just a 21-year-old champion being exactly herself.

This is why tennis is great. 🔥 #WTA #TennisFanrecap #Wimbledon

article.headline: Noskova Said "Let Me Get Drunk" on Live TV. Tennis Twitter Loved It.

research.facts:
Noskova Viral Moment + Post-Wimbledon Plans
**Decision:** FOLLOW UP (Wimbledon women's final covered 2026-07-12). New angle: the viral interview moment and off-court goals not previously covered.
**Sources:**
- Yahoo Sports, Jul 13, 2026: "Linda Noskova said 'let me get drunk'" — primary source for viral quote, full context of Tennis Channel interview
- Puntodebreak.com, Jul 13, 2026: "Noskova after the conquest of Wimbledon reveals what her main goal is outside of tennis" — environmental work, recycling, community goals
- Just Women's Sports: Rankings context for Noskova's new No. 7 ranking
**Key Facts:**
- "Let me get drunk" quote — Tennis Channel post-match interview, confirmed viral (Yahoo Sports — HIGH)
- Reaction and comparison to Sabalenka's similar post-title remarks (Yahoo Sports context — HIGH)
- Off-court goals: environmental protection, recycling, helping communities in different countries (Puntodebreak — HIGH, direct player statement)
- Noskova career-high No. 7 (ranked up 5 spots) — context for why her platform matters now (HIGH)
**Freshness:** Interview aired post-final July 12; went viral July 12-13; Puntodebreak goals article published July 13. Fully fresh.

stories.title: Sinner's Next Chapter: Coach Cahill Confirms Canadian Open, Cincinnati Targeting

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Sinner's next move after Wimbledon:

Canada is on the schedule. Cincinnati targeted. No clay events.

Coach Cahill: "The M1000 events are incredibly important."

The No. 1 heads to North America in August. 🎾

#ATP #Sinner #TennisFanrecap

article.headline: Cahill Confirms: Sinner Is Heading to Canada and Cincinnati After Wimbledon

research.facts:
Sinner's Post-Wimbledon Schedule
**Decision:** NEW STORY — first public statement from Sinner's team about schedule plans.
**Sources:**
- Puntodebreak.com, Jul 12, 2026: "Cahill reveals Sinner's plans for the Masters 1000 in Canada" — primary source, Cahill quotes
- Tennis365: Context on timing difference vs 2025 calendar
- ATP Tour: Schedule reference for Canadian Open (Montreal/Toronto, August)
**Key Facts:**
- Cahill quote: "There are 3 weeks this year. So it's on the schedule. We'll sit down as a team and work out what his schedule is, but the M1000 events are incredibly important." (Puntodebreak — HIGH)
- Canadian Open confirmed as planned (HIGH)
- Cincinnati Open targeted (MEDIUM — team language is "intention," not confirmed entry)
- Extra week between Wimbledon and Canada vs 2025 makes back-to-back feasible (Tennis365 — HIGH)
- No clay events planned — straight to North American hard court swing (HIGH, per Cahill)
**Freshness:** Cahill statement July 12; indexed July 12-13. Fresh.

stories.title: Clay Season Underway: Bastad R1 Results, Rublev Headlines, Passaro Falls to Qualifier

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
The ATP clay swing is underway.

No. 4 seed Passaro fell in Bastad R1 to Czech qualifier Martin Krumich, 6-4, 6-3. Rublev headlines the field.

Gstaad and Umag are running simultaneously. Three clay courts. One week. Tennis doesn't stop.

#ATP #TennisFanrecap #Bastad

article.headline: Passaro Falls, Rublev Headlines: The ATP Clay Swing Opens at Bastad

research.facts:
Bastad R1 / Clay Season Underway
**Decision:** FOLLOW UP (July 13 clay season preview story). Now reporting actual R1 results.
**Sources:**
- Las Vegas Sun, Jul 13, 2026: ATP Tour / Nordea Open Bastad results — full R1 scores confirmed
- Wikipedia / ATP Tour: EFG Swiss Open Gstaad draw and field info
- Croatia Open official / Wikipedia: Plava Laguna Croatia Open Umag draw and seeds
**Key Facts (all HIGH for confirmed July 13 results):**
- Krumich (CZE) def. Passaro [4] (ITA) 6-4, 6-3 — Bastad R1 upset (Las Vegas Sun — HIGH)
- Midon [6] def. Marcondes 7-6(7), 7-5 — Bastad R1 (Las Vegas Sun — HIGH)
- Daniel [5] def. Sanchez Jover 6-2, 2-6, 6-2 — Bastad R1 (Las Vegas Sun — HIGH)
- Damas [8] def. Faurel 3-6, 7-6(6), 6-4 — Bastad R1 (Las Vegas Sun — HIGH)
- Andrey Rublev headlines Bastad field (ATP Tour scouting report — HIGH)
- Gstaad: Bublik No. 1 seed / defending champion; Ruud 2-time former champion; top seeds have byes (Wikipedia/ATP Tour — HIGH)
- Umag: Cobolli [1], ADF [2], Etcheverry [3], Arnaldi [4] all have first-round byes (Croatia Open official — HIGH)
- All three events run July 13-19 (ATP Tour — HIGH)
**Freshness:** July 13 results indexed same day. Current as of today (July 14).

stories.title: Berrettini Reveals Chronic Hip Pain — Out of Gstaad and Kitzbühel, Targeting US Open

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Matteo Berrettini revealed he has been dealing with chronic hip pain.

He's out of Gstaad AND Kitzbühel. The clay swing is done for him.

Eyes on Canada and the US Open now. 💪

#ATP #TennisFanrecap #Berrettini

article.headline: Berrettini Reveals Chronic Hip Pain, Withdraws from Gstaad and Kitzbühel

research.facts:
Berrettini Chronic Hip Pain / Withdrawals
**Decision:** NEW STORY — first public disclosure of the chronic hip pain diagnosis.
**Sources:**
- TennisWorldUSA, Jul 13, 2026: "Matteo Berrettini reveals latest injury diagnosis after Wimbledon setback" — primary source for hip pain disclosure and dual withdrawal
- Tennis365: Additional context
**Key Facts:**
- Berrettini revealed chronic hip pain condition (TennisWorldUSA — HIGH)
- Withdrew from EFG Swiss Open Gstaad (this week) — confirmed (HIGH)
- Withdrew from Generali Open Kitzbühel (July 20-25, next week) — confirmed (HIGH)
- Targeting hard-court season: Canadian Open and US Open (August 24) — player statement (HIGH)
**Freshness:** Revealed July 13. Fully fresh.
